name: BookMyShow Ticket Booking — Concurrent-Safe Transactions
scenario: BookMyShow releases concert tickets. Thousands of users try to book simultaneously. Build a production-safe ticket booking system that prevents double-booking using optimistic locking for general seat selection and pessimistic locking for last-available-ticket scenarios. Include audit logging with REQUIRES_NEW and retry logic.
description: Event/Ticket @Entity with @Version + pessimistic lock option. BookingService with @Retryable + @Lock strategy. AuditService with REQUIRES_NEW. @DataJpaTest verifying concurrent booking safety, audit persistence, and rollback behavior.

core.description: Event @Entity: id, name, totalSeats, availableSeats, @Version int version. TicketBooking @Entity: id, eventId, userId, status (CONFIRMED/CANCELLED), bookedAt. Both with appropriate @Column constraints.
EventRepository: standard JpaRepository + findByIdWithLock() using @Lock(PESSIMISTIC_WRITE) + findByIdForOptimistic() using @Lock(OPTIMISTIC_FORCE_INCREMENT) as an alternative.
BookingAuditService with @Transactional(propagation=REQUIRES_NEW): log(eventId, userId, action, success). Always commits independently — even if main transaction rolls back.

BookingService.book(eventId, userId): @Retryable(ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException, maxAttempts=3, backoff=50ms), @Transactional(rollbackFor=Exception.class). Decrements availableSeats, creates TicketBooking, calls audit.log().
BookingService.cancelBooking(bookingId, userId): @Transactional, marks booking CANCELLED, increments availableSeats on Event, logs audit. Throws if booking not found or not owned by userId.

@DataJpaTest (5 tests): (1) successful booking decrements seats, (2) booking sold-out event fails, (3) cancel increments seats, (4) audit log persists even on booking failure, (5) version increments after booking. All 5 pass.

bonus.description: Add a WaitlistService: when event is sold out, add user to a waitlist. When a booking is cancelled, automatically confirm the first waitlist entry using REQUIRES_NEW (so waitlist processing doesn't block cancellation). @DataJpaTest verifying waitlist behavior.

Deadlock-safe bulk booking: BookingService.bookMultiple(userId, List<eventId>) locks events in ascending ID order before booking any. @DataJpaTest with two threads booking in opposite orders — no deadlock.

Add @Scheduled cleanup: BookingService.releaseExpiredHolds() runs every minute, cancels bookings in PENDING status older than 10 minutes, restores seats with REQUIRES_NEW per booking. Demonstrate idempotency.

data_to_use:
Events for @DataJpaTest:
  EVT-001: 'Diljit Dosanjh Live', totalSeats=1000, availableSeats=1000
  EVT-002: 'Sold Out Concert', totalSeats=100, availableSeats=0

Booking flow:
  bookTicket(EVT-001, U001) → availableSeats=999, version=1
  bookTicket(EVT-001, U002) → availableSeats=998, version=2
  bookTicket(EVT-002, U003) → SOLD_OUT exception
  cancelBooking(booking1, U001) → availableSeats=999, version=3
example_output:
# Test run:
✓ bookTicket_success_decrementsSeats
✓ bookTicket_soldOut_throwsException
✓ cancelBooking_incrementsSeats
✓ auditLog_persistsOnBookingFailure
✓ version_incrementsAfterBooking

# Retry log (under concurrent load):
[INFO] @Retryable: attempt 2/3 for event EVT-001 (ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException)
[INFO] Retried successfully on attempt 2

# Audit log entries after test:
EventId=EVT-001, action=BOOKING_SUCCESS, userId=U001, success=true
EventId=EVT-002, action=BOOKING_FAILED,  userId=U003, success=false
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. @Retryable + @Transactional: each retry creates a NEW transaction (previous transaction rolled back on OptimisticLockException before retry). REQUIRES_NEW: audit transaction commits independently — if book() throws, audit still saved. rollbackFor=Exception.class: checked exceptions (if any) also trigger rollback. @Version field: use int/Integer, not Long, for simple version counter. ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException is Spring's exception — catch this, not the raw Hibernate StaleObjectStateException.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. All 5 @DataJpaTest tests pass. README explains: when optimistic vs pessimistic locking was chosen and why. Document the @Retryable settings rationale (3 attempts, 50ms backoff).
tips_for_success: Sold-out check: if event.getAvailableSeats() <= 0, throw IllegalStateException BEFORE decrementing
Audit with REQUIRES_NEW: call audit.log() INSIDE the @Transactional book() method — it runs in its own nested transaction
Cancel restores seats: event.setAvailableSeats(event.getAvailableSeats() + 1) — also needs @Version protection
@Retryable: each retry is a fresh @Transactional — re-reads the entity with latest version
testEm.flush() before assertions: forces pending SQL to execute before reading back
concepts_reinforced: Optimistic locking with @Version for low-contention concurrent updates
@Retryable for transparent retry on optimistic lock conflict
Pessimistic locking with @Lock PESSIMISTIC_WRITE for high-contention critical sections
Propagation.REQUIRES_NEW for independent audit log transaction
Lost update prevention in concurrent ticket booking scenario
